key_points: Carl Sandburg’s poem 'Fog' presents a vivid description of fog using imaginative comparison
The fog is portrayed as a cat that moves silently and settles quietly
The poem highlights the quiet arrival, presence, and departure of fog
The harbour and city symbolize the places silently covered by fog
The poem demonstrates the poet’s keen observation of simple natural phenomena
characters: Fog – A symbolic representation of silent natural movement
Cat (metaphorical figure) – Symbolizes stealth, observation, and graceful motion
themes: Observation of simple yet meaningful natural events
Appreciation of the calm and mysterious aspects of nature
literary_devices: Metaphor – The fog is compared to a cat to emphasize stealth and silence
Imagery – The poem paints a vivid visual picture of fog spreading and settling
Personification – Fog is given the ability to sit and look like a living creature
exam_tips: Explain how the metaphor strengthens the poem’s imagery
Discuss the symbolic meaning of the cat in relation to fog
Focus on how the poem shows the quiet beauty of nature
Mention the use of free verse and absence of rhyme scheme
